Now speaking

The written briefing

World

  • Middle East — update. At the NATO summit in Ankara, Trump said the memorandum with Iran is "over" and called talks "a waste of time"; Iran's Revolutionary Guard declared the Strait of Hormuz closed as the US ran a fresh round of strikes. Al Jazeera · Haaretz
  • World Cup — update. The semis are set with the top four FIFA-ranked sides all through (a first): France v Spain today (Dallas), England v Argentina Wed (Atlanta). Al Jazeera · ESPN
  • Science: a new study suggests the brain begins committing to a decision earlier than previously thought — before conscious awareness of the choice. ScienceDaily

AI & Tech

  • GPT-Live — OpenAI's new full-duplex voice model listens, reasons and speaks at once, with real-time translation and live web search mid-conversation. Tech Startups
  • Gemini 3.5 Pro (rumor). Leaks point to a July 17 GA with a 2M-token context window — unconfirmed by Google. BigGo
  • Anthropic tripled its Glasswing security program to 150 organizations, pointing Claude at code that runs hospitals, utilities and banks. Tech Startups

Weather — Athens

  • Hot and sunny, a touch cooler than yesterday. Low 20s in the morning, near 34°C in the afternoon, mid-20s by evening. Northerly meltemi winds keep it breezy but raise fire risk; minimal rain. Sunset ~20:45. easeWeather

Greece

  • Fire alert. Meltemi winds plus heat have crews on high alert across Attica; a weekend blaze hit central Athens. Avoid dry brush and open flame. Greek City Times

Personal & Communication

  • Calendar connector unavailable — check it manually. One travel item noted for Friday.
  • 3 inbox items worth attention: 1 payment/account action, 1 subscription auto-renewing Thu, 1 new-device sign-in to glance at. Everything else is receipts and noise.

Gaming & Deals

  • Epic free games — last call. Nova Lands and Tattoo Tycoon leave Wed ~18:00 Athens; next up is the horror game Luto (plus Echo Generation: Midnight Edition). Yardbarker · Notebookcheck

Learning

  • Word of the day — beta: in climbing, the exact sequence of moves that solves a route. "She gave me the beta for the crux and it finally clicked."
  • Surprising fact: an octopus has three hearts, and two of them stop beating whenever it swims — which is why it generally prefers to crawl. Natural History Museum
